prose:
Property: atom-resolved Fukui indices (electrophilic f+ and nucleophilic f−) and dual descriptors for furan.
Level of theory: DFT tier `standard` (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, RI approximation on) in PySCF.
Geometry provenance: fixed; single-point population analysis executed on the user-supplied /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/furan/furan.xyz structure (no re-optimization).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent model).
Charge and multiplicity: neutral singlet (charge 0, multiplicity 1). Radical cation and anion states used for N±1 runs were charge +1, multiplicity 2 and charge −1, multiplicity 2, respectively.
Software: chemkit fukui-reactivity skill → PySCF back-end (version as in engine). Mulliken population scheme.
Convergence & warnings: SCF for N, N+1, and N−1 states all reported converged; the integrity block carries three warnings reproduced verbatim below.

Most electrophilic site (largest f+): atom 5 (C, 0-based index 4) with f+ = 1.9242931.
Most nucleophilic site (largest f−): atom 5 (same C) with f− = 0.1616371.
Headline number reported above (value field) is this largest f+.

Live log: the run streamed to a file announced during execution (path retained in the CLI invocation recorded in the JSON).

Verbatim warnings from the tool result:
• sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• common header present

integrity.trustworthy verdict: true.
Provenance: computed (no experimental data involved).
